name: Terracotta Print-Proof Studio Portfolio Landing
description: A warm Mediterranean studio portfolio on a plaster-grain terracotta canvas where project image cards overlap at 2–4° rotations with drop shadows that simulate physical prints stacked on a table, hand-drawn SVG underlines beneath section titles that wobble slightly like dried ink, a serif/sans type pairing where the serif (an old-style face) is reserved exclusively for proper nouns and project names while everything else is set in a light geometric sans, registration marks and crop-mark crosses positioned at the corners of each panel like a print proof awaiting trimming, and a footer contact block set in small-caps tracked monospace on a torn-edge paper-white strip.

vibes.mood: Feels like picking up a stack of printed proofs from a Lisbon studio's worktable — the terracotta grain is dried plaster dust, the crop marks say this was prepared for press, and the hand-drawn underlines are the creative director's pen corrections on the final galley.
influences: aesop store bag and packaging typography
pre-press proof sheets with registration marks

meta.audience: Architecture firms, hospitality brands, and luxury goods clients who evaluate a studio by whether the portfolio itself feels like a designed object — people who notice crop marks and know what they mean
